calendar.matters:
Petition For Apportionment Of Attorneys Fees And Costs; Retraction Of Prior Waiver Of Fees; Etc.
Off Calendar Oct-02-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Marshall Wallace for (administrator/petitioner) Lulu Bagaman.

Mr. Wallace reported he spoke with Mr. Bresler in the hallway this morning, that Mr. Bresler stated he was ill and could stay, that he handed Mr. Wallace a document stipulating to judgment on the pleadings and confirmed that he is in agreement regarding fees. The Court stated that it will accept Mr. Wallace's interpretation, that this matter can go forward, confirmed with the clerk that Mr. Bresler did not check in. The Court stated that the issues are the allocation of ordinary fees and how much extraordinary fees Mr. Bresler and the Allen Matkins firm are entitled to, that some of it includes time for ordinary fees and asked if the $10,000.00 already paid to the Allen Matkins firm was out of the estate. Mr. Wallace stated that it was paid out of Ms. Bagaman's personal funds and that she is not asking for reimbursement, that the request for extraordinary fees was included to catch any overage, that the statutory fees were over $20,000.00, that they needed to properly cover the overage, that they are really talking about $4,000.00, that they understand their rates are higher than others and that a lot of work went into this. The petition for apportionment of attorney's fees and costs and the accounting petition were taken under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputies Watts & Siu. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar May-12-2015 Continued To Jul-14-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: .

Petition For Apportionment Of Attorneys Fees And Costs; Retraction Of Prior Waiver Of Fees; Etc.
Off Calendar May-12-2015 Continued To Jul-14-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Marshall Wallace for (Administrator) Lulu Bagaman. Peter Bresler (Former Counsel) in Propria Persona. Nell Clement for (Objector) Francis Bagaman.

The Court stated that there are 2 petitions, that it appears that the estate cannot fully cover the statutory or extraordinary fees, asks if it is necessary to go ahead with determining the amount of Francis' debt, that there are attorney fee issues, administration issues, proper records have not been kept, expenditures are not sufficiently documented, that there is an over pay of $13,000.00 to the administrator as well as other issues raised in the objections. Mr. Wallace reported that he has reached out to Mr. Bresler & Ms. Clement, has presented the accounting to the objector as well as a detailed explanation regarding the funds, that advance notice was not given, that everything was done in good faith by the administrator, that the administrator's proposition is to waive the debt and that he is not sure what else he can do to informally resolve the issues. Ms. Clement stated that the debt issue came up in the first petition, that there are concerns regarding the money loaned and repaid without notification, that there has been over $100,000.00 in cash payments with no receipts or explanations and that Ms. Bagaman should provide receipts. The Court stated that Ms. Bagaman has supported what has been done with testimony and that she has no receipts. Ms. Clement stated that the contractor should provide a declaration and that she would hope the court would require her to provide evidence. The Court suggested the parties get together and talk about the issues or agree to go to mediation. Mr. Wallace stated that the objector has had 6 months to come up with any discovery, that the petition put forth ample evidence, that this hearing has been continued multiple times and that there is no money in the estate. Mr. Wallace & Ms. Clement agreed to Pro Bono Mediation and that the fee allocation issue should be held for a later time. Mr. Bresler requested to be kept out of mediation but would like to be kept advised of proceedings. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than July 7, 2015. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by July 7, 2015. The matter was continued to and the Parties are to return on July 14, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation. The Petition for Apportionment of attorneys fees, etc., and the accounting petition were continued to that date also.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Lopes. (Not Reported)
